claim_status: No-claim platform accessibility-source observations only; platform API documentation is not native accessibility readiness, screen-reader coverage, IME correctness, page-tree composition proof, UI-GATE evidence, or release-path UI approval.

sources.title: Accessibility for Windows apps
sources.observation: Windows accessibility guidance describes semantic controls and platform accessibility behavior that must be exposed to users and assistive technologies.
sources.evidence_consequence: Record Windows build, framework/API identity, control semantics, keyboard/focus behavior, high contrast/forced color state, and exact assistive-technology configuration.

sources.title: UI Automation overview
sources.observation: UI Automation exposes an accessibility tree, control patterns, properties, events, and client interaction behavior; an internal semantic model alone is not a UI Automation result.
sources.evidence_consequence: Retain platform tree snapshots, roles/properties/control patterns, focus and event traces, action results, timing, client/version identity, and failure or unsupported rows.

sources.title: Accessibility
sources.observation: Apple accessibility documentation defines platform API and assistive-technology integration surfaces, including the exposed accessibility hierarchy and actions.
sources.evidence_consequence: Record macOS version, accessibility API/runtime, VoiceOver version and configuration, hierarchy/actions, focus/live-region behavior, text/selection results, timing, and unsupported cases.

sources.title: AT-SPI 2 API
sources.observation: AT-SPI exposes accessible objects, actions, components, documents, text, selections, states, relations, and events through a runtime accessibility bus.
sources.evidence_consequence: Record distribution/session, AT-SPI runtime and bus state, Orca version/configuration, object tree and relations, states/events, text/selection behavior, timeout handling, and unsupported cases.

sources.title: Complete guide to Narrator
sources.observation: The Windows Narrator guide documents keyboard and scan-mode navigation, reading and interaction behavior, configuration, and supported language or input considerations for a built-in screen reader.
sources.evidence_consequence: Record Windows build, Narrator guide/configuration revision, scan or navigation mode, keyboard/braille settings where used, spoken transcript, focus and announcement timing, and every unsupported or ambiguous interaction.

sources.title: VoiceOver User Guide
sources.observation: Apple's VoiceOver guide defines the keyboard and interaction model used to navigate Mac applications and web content with speech and related assistive controls.
sources.evidence_consequence: Record macOS and VoiceOver versions, interaction/navigation mode, keyboard or braille configuration, manual transcript, focus/announcement behavior, rotor or equivalent navigation state, and unsupported workflows.

sources.title: Orca Screen Reader
sources.observation: GNOME's Orca guide covers structural navigation, forms, live regions, object review, profiles, and keyboard commands for speech and refreshable-braille interaction.
sources.evidence_consequence: Record distribution/session, Orca and AT-SPI versions, profile and keymap, structural-navigation mode, manual transcript or braille action record, event timing, live-region behavior, and unsupported workflows.

unsupported: No reference-platform accessibility workflow has been executed.
No platform accessibility tree snapshot or manual screen-reader transcript has been reviewed.
No page/chrome composed accessibility tree, IME correctness, or UI-GATE-10 evidence exists.
No accessibility readiness, release-path UI approval, production, or implementation claim exists.
